architecture.summary: The system fine-tunes a language model around a fixed sparse autoencoder (SAE) by adding an orthogonality regularization penalty to the SAE's decoder matrix. This penalty encourages the learned features (decoder columns) to become nearly orthogonal, reducing interference and superposition. The orthogonalized features are then used for causal intervention experiments, where individual features are modified and the model's output is observed. The seam between the language model and the SAE is instrumented via the SAE's learned latent activations, which are regularized during training.
trained_components: SAE decoder matrix with orthogonality regularization

biblio.title: Identifying Intervenable and Interpretable Features via Orthogonality Regularization

claims.statement: Orthogonality penalty leads to identifiable features, ensuring the uniqueness of the decomposition
The distance between embedded feature explanations increases with stricter orthogonality penalty, a desirable property for interpretability
Orthogonality reduces interference and superposition between features while keeping performance on the target dataset essentially unchanged
Increasingly orthogonalized features allow for isolated interventions
Invoking the Independent Causal Mechanisms principle, orthogonality promotes modular representations amenable to causal intervention
